items.title: MNIST digits colored by class in 2D UMAP space
items.scenario: A practitioner runs UMAP on flattened 784-pixel MNIST images to get 60k points in 2D, then scatter-plots with one color per digit 0–9. Digits 4 and 9 partially overlap while 0 and 1 form tight islands—useful EDA before building a classifier.
items.explanation: The plot reveals which classes share local geometry; overlap hints at confusable pairs to inspect in a confusion matrix, while tight islands suggest easier separation.

items.title: Coloring by model prediction to find systematic errors
items.scenario: On a text embedding UMAP of product reviews, points are colored by true sentiment (green/red) but misclassified reviews are drawn with an X marker. A red cluster inside the green region shows the model treats short neutral phrases as positive.
items.explanation: Dual encoding (color = truth, marker = error) turns the embedding into a qualitative error-analysis dashboard linked to classifier debugging.

items.title: 3D interactive plot for stakeholder demos
items.scenario: A genomics team exports PCA scores to three components and builds a rotatable 3D Plotly scatter colored by tissue type for a lab meeting. Reviewers can spin the view to see whether a suspected outlier cluster is real or projection artifact.
items.explanation: 3D plus interactivity helps when two dimensions hide structure; still document that angles and depth cues can exaggerate separation.

items.title: Hexbin density when n = 200k points
items.scenario: Plotting 200k user embeddings as raw scatter produces a gray smear. The analyst switches to hexbin colored by count per cell, then overlays centroids of k-means clusters computed in the original high-dimensional space.
items.explanation: Density-aware visualization prevents overplotting; combining density with cluster centroids links visual structure to formal clustering without claiming the hex bins are clusters.
